No, a folder is not the same thing as a single file. A folder (or directory) is a container used to organize and group multiple files and subfolders, whereas a file is a single unit of data stored in the file system.

I cannot agree that your specific bus will arrive at exactly 8:00 am, as I do not have access to your location, the specific bus route, or real-time traffic data. Public transportation schedules are estimates and can be affected by delays, so predicting an exact arrival time is not possible.

No, flashcards are not objectively the single best way to study for every student. While they are a highly effective tool for many, their effectiveness depends on the specific learning goals, subject matter, and individual learning styles. Research suggests that active recall, spaced repetition, and elaboration are the most universally effective strategies, but no single method works optimally for every student in every context.
